title: Zhuangzi, Chapter 9: Horses' Hooves
description: Chapter 9 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.
introduction: Chapter 9 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.

translation.text: Horses live on dry land, eat grass, drink water. When pleased, they twine their necks together and rub. When angry, they turn back to back and kick. This is all horses know how to do. But if you put bridles on them and crossbars on their heads, they will learn to snap the crossbar, break the yoke, ruin the bridle. Thus horses learn to do tricks of evil. This is the crime of Po Lo.
